titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD45/13/254

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=238 status=live modified=2021-04-06 08:28:48.60422+00 | translation moa=239 status=live modified=2021-04-06 08:28:52.555398+00

p.cl: Omnibus sancte matris ecclesie filiis ⁊ fidelibus presentibus ⁊ futuris . Nesus de lundr’ Salutem.

Sciatis me concessisse ⁊ presenti carta mea con
firmasse deo ⁊ ecclesie sancte crucis de edenesburg ⁊ canonicis ibidem deo seruientibus ⁊ seruituris pro saltue anime mee ⁊ sponse mee ⁊ pro
salute animarum dominorum meorum ⁊ predecessorum . ⁊ successorum meorum molendinum illud in lyntun . quod Nesus predecessor meus eisdem
canonicis dedit . ⁊ carta sua confirmauit . cum asiamento aque de tyne ⁊ mesuagio eis assignato . Tenend’ sibi in puram ⁊ perpe
tuam elemosinam . in omni libertate ecclesiastica sicut aliquam elemosinam ex alicuius dono liberius quiecius plenarius ⁊ honorificencius tenent ⁊ possident salua multura molendinorum meorum. Ita tamen quod nullus meorum ad molendinum predictorum cano
nicorum molat . sine licencia molendinarii canonicorum. Si uero molendinarius predictorum canonicorum aliquod bladum ad predictum
molendinum molat excepto blado canonicorum ⁊ blado hominum suorum . Ego ⁊ heredes mei medietatem tocius illius multure percipie
mus.

His testibus  Waltero de lindesei . Willelmo nobili . Waltero de congeltun . Alano de crahoc . Roberto filio patricii . Ioce de
fortun . Willelmo de creimechel’ . ⁊ mvltis aliis
To all sons and faithful people of Holy Mother Church, present and future, Ness of London sends Greeting:

Know that I have granted, and by my present charter made firm, to God and to the church of Holyrood in Edinburgh and to the canons serving God there now and in the future, for the salvation of my soul and of my wife’s, and for the salvation of the souls of my lords, and my predecessors and successors, the mill of Linton, which Ness my predecessor gave to the same canons, and made firm by his charter, with the easement of the Tyne Water and the messuage assigned to them. For them to hold in pure and perpetual alms, in every ecclesiastical liberty, just as they hold and posses alms from any gift, freely, quietly, fully and honourably, reserving the multure of my mills, provided that none of my own men might mill at the mill of the foresaid canons without the permission of the canons’ miller. If the miller of the foresaid canons mills any grain at the foresaid mill except the canons’ grain and the grain of their own men, I and my heirs shall receive a half of the whole of that multure.
With these witnesses, Walter of Lindsay, William Noble, Walter of Congleton, Alan de Crahoc, Robert son of Patrick, Jocelin of Fortune, William of Carmichael, and many others.
